requirement: Buyer can initiate the supplier onboarding process by providing basic supplier information.
Supplier onboarding process undergoes internal approval workflow.
Approved supplier receives invitation to register with AECI and complete due diligence steps.
Supplier information is accurate and current.
Supplier certifications and contact information are visible.
Multiple channels of managing supplier information are eliminated.
Supplier is created in Coupa only after completing the onboarding process.
Supplier status can be managed, including purchase block, payment block, deactivation, and reactivation.
Internal users can register new suppliers in Coupa for urgent or business-critical situations, adhering to governance and approval controls.
Internal users can update existing supplier information in Coupa, following the same approval workflow as new supplier registration.
Internal users can change the status of a supplier in Coupa, including blocking purchase orders or payments, deactivating, or reactivating the supplier.
Internal users can create a one-time vendor in Coupa with a simplified approval flow and reduced data requirements, while ensuring basic compliance and procurement controls.
Users can view the status of each supplier in Coupa, including the supplier record status and Coupa Supplier Portal status.
The system will display geography-specific field requirements and attachments based on the selected country for the supplier.
The system will support auto-translation of standard information on the user interface for multiple languages, and allow for custom translation configuration.
Process owners and document reviewers can acknowledge and sign the document.
